++Deck'd Out 2023 Summer Series++
Come experience outdoor dancing with a view of the skyline at one of Seattle's most beloved house/techno venues in the city. Deck'd Out is an early evening, sunset rooftop party from 7p-11p every Thursday this summer.
Each week we'll be showcasing two stages of music with rotating DJs/crews from the Pacific Northwest and beyond.

Deck'd Out #10 - August 25th - Portland Edition

We love our neighbors to the South and Seattle doesn't get a chance to catch their vibes up here very often. For this particular edition of Deck'd Out, we've invited Natural Magic to take over the rooftop for 3 hours. We've also invited back Believe You Me's Ginkgo for a special Yo Yo Yoga DJ set that will lead right into Deck'd Out. We hope you'll come support some of what Portland has to offer and show them that Seattle \u003C3. 

+++Natural Magic+++
(Boomarm Nation/Portland, OR)
Natural Magic are Portland, OR based duo known for throwing discreet Klipschorn powered all-night parties. Last year they released a cassette of original material on Boomarm Nation and more recently, remixes for Krycek, Warm Hands and Apartment Fox. They like to DJ for a really, really long time.
https://www.facebook.com/naturalmagicdjs/
https://soundcloud.com/thenaturalmagic

+++Ginkgo+++
(Believe You Me/Portland, OR)
https://soundcloud.com/ginkgo

Happy Hour 7-9p

Plus, Yo Yo Yoga beforehand from 5:30-7p with Audrey Dohm and Ginkgo. Free cover to Deck'd Out with Yo Yo Yoga ticket.

Stretch then DANCE!

Bodywise Massage will be on deck as well. 15 minute massage for $20. 

Thursday August 4th
7-11p 
Monkey Loft
2915 1st ave S
21+, $5 (or free w/ your Shameless sunnies)

+++Cascadia Prefunk Edition+++

Starborne Presents Cascadia Festival : a summertime celebration featuring 4 nights & 3 days of camping with live music, DJs, dancing, art, workshops, performances, artisan vending & the spirit of community. Cascadia is held at the Masonic Family Park, a beatiful campground location nestled in the woods, next to a river, in Granite Falls, WA, just 75 minutes north of Seattle.

About Lee Reynolds (Desert Hearts, LA)
Originally from England, Lee Reynolds moved to California in 1988 to ride BMX bikes. After numerous injuries he switched his focus from one pair of wheels to another, and started DJing in '92, quickly beginning to throw parties in the San Diego area, and focusing on the ambient and psychedelic sounds of the time.. an influence that will always remain.

Not content with just chillin' out the newly dubbed 'Dr.Indulgent' began to explore the more leftfield areas of dance music, collecting weird records on frequent trips back to London, playing gigs up and down the West Coast, and appearing on early internet radio shows such as Groovetech.

Fast forward to 2014 and Lee is heavily involved in the Southern California tech/house movement, co-hosting the popular Desert Hearts, Tropical Hearts, Moonshake & Tribalove parties, playing at the legendary Moontribe and Lightning in a Bottle events, and often DJing south of the border. With over 20 years of experience under his belt Lee has never had more fun DJing!!!
https://soundcloud.com/lee_reynolds
http://www.residentadvisor.net/dj/leereynolds
https://www.facebook.com/djleereynolds

Hard to believe we're nearing our 10th Deck'd Out sunset session as the summer seems to have flown by. This week we're honored to host some of KEXP's most influential players. Each DJ has made a name for themselves and have been holding it down in Seattle for years. In fact, they ARE Seattle. And we have each of them to thank for their musical taste on both the airwaves and dance floors. Thrilled to have all three together for this event. 

And...as always.... ;) what makes this even more fun is that we get to invite to you to join us for FREE! No sponsors, no \"guest lists\" that close early, no email sign up trickery...just your pretty face and summer lovin' vibe. So tell your friends and lets come together to celebrate our beautiful city from the Monkey Roof! Gotta get it while you can.

About Natural Magic:

Holding down the outsider-balearic-psychedelic-dubbed out-heavy frequencies Portland chapter, Natural Magic are Pacific Northwest nice guys Mike McKinnon and Matthew Quiet. Their infamous Limited Edition parties are intimate, Klipschorn powered quarterly affairs featuring one DJ, all night. Curators of sacred moments and emotions, they are irreverent crowd-pleasers with a third ear towards the sunrise.

https://soundcloud.com/thenaturalmagic
https://www.facebook.com/naturalmagicdjs

About Shameless Productions:

Shameless first took shape as a weekly indie dance night in the basement of the Alibi Room, located in Seattle's historic Pike Place Market. Shortly thereafter, Shameless Productions formed and immediately began focusing on emerging electronic music and multimedia performance. The crew quickly became one of the city's most respected underground dance music collectives by throwing numerous legendary club nights and after parties. From day one, each Shameless party was a special one, despite the wide range of genres and bookings represented. With an eye towards the cutting edge and a deep respect for electronic music's rich history, Shameless has kept its finger on the pulse of Seattle's underground for over 12 years, while continuously looking forward.

Beatport Release: Joe Bellingham's 'Our Reflection Blues' EP
Official Beatport debut of Shameless Audio 001 - Joe Bellingham's Our Reflection Blues EP
The LAST Shameless Social - W/ Dirt Crew's James Flavour and Jeff Samuel!

It's true. After close to 4 years of 4th Saturday events, we're finally taking a break from our monthly. Although it doesn't mean that the Shameless Social won't return again down the road. We love the Re-bar and of course the concept of bringing various scenes together each month for a late night get down. But with the launch of our record label Shameless Audio and busy Shameless resident DJ/producers working in the studio/playing gigs, the long running monthly will taking a sabbatical. We'll still be throwing one offs and our series of various annual events of course so don't think this is the LAST of the last. In fact, expect more exciting Shameless news to come!

For our final Social session, Shameless invites you to a special collaboration with Peloton on hosting not one, but two great international headliners, the Dirt Crew's James Flavour and Seattle-to-Berlin transplant Jeff Samuel.  Come early and stay late, coz it's gonna be a good one!

Shameless Audio Record Label Launch Party!

Shameless is proud to present a very special live set from...

LUSINE 
(Ghostly International) [LIVE]

Jeff McIlwain has been producing his visceral, melodic strain of abstract electronic music as Lusine for over 10 years now. Originally a Texas native, McIlwain met Shad Scott while living in LA and put out his self titled debut on Scott's imprint, Isophlux. He relocated to Seattle in late 2002 and began steadily releasing his music on Ghostly International. McIlwain has also contributed tracks to various compilations and remix releases on Mute, !K7, Kompakt, Asthmatic Kitty, and Shitkatapult. In 2009, he released the album, \"A Certain Distance\", which featured the single, "Two Dots." His newest, critically acclaimed album, \"The Waiting Room\", was just recently released in 2013.
McIlwain has performed throughout the US and abroad, including the Sasquatch, SXSW, Mutek, DEMF, and Sonar festivals, and sets at London's esteemed Fabric nightclub, Tokyo's Unit, and Berlin's Berghain and Watergate nightclubs. He has been involved in the scoring of various film and commercial projects: co-scoring (with David Wingo) the 2008 film \"Snow Angels\" (with Kate Beckinsale and Sam Rockwell), and \"The Sitter\" (Jonah Hill) and scoring the 2009 film \"Linewatch\" (with Cuba Gooding Jr. and Omari Hardwick). McIlwain teamed up with David Wingo again this year to score the film \"Joe\" (Nicolas Cage) . He is planning a few more US live dates and preparing for a European tour over the next few months in 2013.
